title: GitHub Copilot app adds bring-your-own-key model providers
friendlyTitle: GitHub Copilot can now use your own AI keys
summary: GitHub says the Copilot app now supports bring your own key, letting users run agent sessions against OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Microsoft Foundry, Anthropic, LM Studio, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Configured models appear beside Copilot-hosted models, while credentials are stored locally in the operating-system keychain.
friendlySummary: The Copilot app can connect to model providers you already use, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Ollama, LM Studio, and OpenAI-compatible services.
whyItMatters: BYOK turns coding agents into a more flexible control plane: teams can route work through their own providers, tenants, local models, or internal gateways. That matters for cost management, data boundaries, regional controls, and regulated workflows where the default hosted model is not always acceptable.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This gives teams more control over which AI model handles coding work. It can help with privacy, budget, and company rules.
